name: Instagram
antiBot: aggressive rate-limits, device fingerprinting, and behavioral action-blocks
scrapingContext: Instagram couples every request to a logged-in session and a device fingerprint, so agencies and growth tools route each managed account through its own sticky mobile or residential IP to avoid action-blocks and phone-verification challenges. Public scraping of profiles, hashtags, and Reels metadata is throttled the moment requests pattern-match automation, making clean IP reputation essential.
successNote: Assigning one consistent mobile IP per account and pacing actions to human speed is what keeps Instagram sessions alive long-term.

name: TikTok
antiBot: signed request parameters, device attestation, and regional rate-limits
scrapingContext: TikTok signs its API requests and inspects device and network signals, so scraping video metadata, trends, and creator stats at scale requires residential or mobile exits that match the target region. Marketers running multiple creator or ad accounts rotate mobile IPs to dodge shadowbans and login checkpoints tied to shared datacenter ranges.
successNote: Geo-matched mobile IPs that mirror a real handset are what let TikTok requests slip past device attestation.
